message.content:
I need to understand the ticket system's wizard and update functionality.

Search for:
1. The action_open_as_wizard method in ticket.py - understand how it opens the popup
2. How updates are added to tickets (mail.activity.update model)
3. Any existing patterns in the codebase where lists have both editable="bottom" AND a way to open a detailed popup (like an icon column or action)
4. Check if there are any list views that use widgets or special fields to add action buttons/links per row

Focus on:
- X:\03_ACG\Custom_Addons\ticket_system\models\ticket.py
- X:\03_ACG\Custom_Addons\ticket_system\views\
- Look for patterns with "editable" lists that also have row-level actions

Report back with code snippets showing how these work together.

content.text: I'll search for these elements in the ticket system. Let me start by exploring the key files and patterns.

content.content:
1 from odoo import models, fields, api 2 from .graph_api import GraphAPIClient 3 import logging 4 _logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) 5 6 class MailActivityTag(models.Model): 7 """ 8 Simple Categorization: 9 Defines tags like 'Urgent', 'IT', or 'Maintenance' so users can 10 quickly filter the ticket list. 11 """ 12 _name = 'mail.activity.tag' 13 _description = 'Activity Tag' 14 15 name = fields.Char(string='Tag Name', required=True) 16 color = fields.Integer(string='Color Index') 17 18 class MailActivityUpdate(models.Model): 19 """ 20 Audit Log: 21 A permanent record of progress notes. This is where the notes 22 from the wizard end up after the wizard itself is deleted. 23 """ 24 _name = 'mail.activity.update' 25 _description = 'Activity Update Log' 26 _order = 'date desc' 27 28 activity_id = fields.Many2one( 29 'mail.activity', 30 string='Activity', 31 required=True, 32 ondelete='cascade' 33 ) 34 date = fields.Datetime( 35 string='Date', 36 default=fields.Datetime.now, 37 required=True 38 ) 39 user_id = fields.Many2one( 40 'res.users', 41 string='User', 42 default=lambda self: self.env.user, 43 required=True 44 ) 45 note = fields.Html(string='Update Note') 46 attachment_ids = fields.Many2many( 47 'ir.attachment', 48 'mail_activity_update_attachment_rel', 49 'update_id', 50 'attachment_id', 51 string='Attachments', 52 help='Attach multiple files to this update' 53 ) 54 notify_creator = fields.Boolean( 55 string='Notify Ticket Creator', 56 default=True, 57 help='Send email notification to the person who created this ticket' 58 ) 59 60 @api.model_create_multi 61 def create(self, vals_list): 62 updates = super().create(vals_list) 63 64 for update in updates: 65 if update.notify_creator and update.activity_id.create_uid: 66 try: 67 graph_client = GraphAPIClient(self.env) 68 creator = update.activity_id.create_uid 69 70 if creator.email and creator != update.user_id: 71 subject = f"Update on Ticket: {update.activity_id.summary or 'No Subject'}" 72 base_url = self.env['ir.config_parameter'].sudo().get_param('web.base.url') 73 action_url = f"{base_url}/web#id={update.activity_id.id}&model=mail.activity&view_type=form" 74 75 body = f""" 76
77
78

Ticket Update Notification

79
80 81
82

Hello {creator.name},

83

An update has been added to a ticket you created:

84 85 86 87 88 89 90 91 92 93 94 95 96 97 98
Ticket:{update.activity_id.summary or 'No Summary'}
Updated By:{update.user_id.name}
Date:{update.date}
99 100
101 Update Note:
102
103 {update.note or 'No notes provided.'} 104
105
106 107

Please click the button below to view the ticket and respond if needed.

108 109
110 111 View Ticket in Odoo 112 113
114
115 116
117 This is an automated notification from the ACG Ticket System. 118
119
120 """ 121 122 graph_client.send_mail( 123 recipient_email=creator.email, 124 subject=subject, 125 content=body 126 ) 127 except Exception as e: 128 _logger.error("Failed to send update notification: %s", str(e)) 129 130 return updates 131 132 133 class ActivityGroup(models.Model): 134 """ 135 Organizational Buckets: 136 Used to group activities into logical sections like 'Phase 1' 137 or 'Onboarding Stages'. 138 """ 139 _name = "acg.activity.group" 140 _description = "Activity Group" 141 _order = "name" 142 143 name = fields.Char(required=True, index=True) 144 active = fields.Boolean(default=True) 145 notes = fields.Text() 146 147 class MailActivity(models.Model): 148 """ 149 The Core Ticket Model: 150 Inherits Odoo's standard activity system and transforms it 151 into a departmental ticketing tool. 152 """ 153 _inherit = 'mail.activity' 154 155 target_department_id = fields.Many2one( 156 'hr.department', 157 string="Target Department" 158 ) 159 160 target_document_item_id = fields.Many2one( 161 'document.library.item', 162 string = "Target Document" 163 ) 164 165 additional_document_ids = fields.Many2many( 166 'document.library.item', 167 'mail_activity_document_rel', 168 'activity_id', 169 'document_id', 170 string="Additional Documents", 171 help="Select multiple documents related to this activity" 172 ) 173 174 activity_tag_ids = fields.Many2many( 175 'mail.activity.tag', 176 string='Tags' 177 ) 178 update_line_ids = fields.One2many( 179 'mail.activity.update', 180 'activity_id', 181 string="Updates" 182 ) 183 184 certification_name_id = fields.Many2many( 185 'hr.certification.name', 186 string="Certification Name" 187 ) 188 189 group_id = fields.Many2one( 190 "acg.activity.group", 191 string="Group Name", 192 index=True, 193 ) 194 195 plan_template_id = fields.Many2one( 196 "mail.activity.plan.template", 197 string="Activity Plan Template", 198 index=True, 199 readonly=True 200 ) 201 202 priority = fields.Selection( 203 selection=[ 204 ('p1', 'P1'), 205 ('p2', 'P2'), 206 ('p3', 'P3'), 207 ], 208 string='Priority', 209 ) 210 211 @api.onchange('target_department_id') 212 def _onchange_target_department(self): 213 """ 214 Smart Routing: 215 When a department is picked, automatically set the activity 216 to track against that specific department record. 217 """ 218 if self.target_department_id: 219 self.res_model_id = self.env['ir.model']._get_id('hr.department') 220 self.res_id = self.target_department_id.id 221 222 @api.model_create_multi 223 def create(self, vals_list): 224 225 """ 226 Creation Override: 227 1. Inherits logic from Plan Templates. 228 2. Saves the record to the database. 229 3. Triggers Microsoft Graph API Email notifications. 230 """ 231 # --- INHERIT FROM TEMPLATE --- 232 template_ids = {v.get("plan_template_id") for v in vals_list if v.get("plan_template_id")} 233 templates = {} 234 if template_ids: 235 recs = self.env["mail.activity.plan.template"].browse(list(template_ids)).exists() 236 templates = {t.id: t for t in recs} 237 238 for v in vals_list: 239 tmpl = templates.get(v.get("plan_template_id")) 240 if not tmpl: 241 continue 242 243 # if any value missing, pull from template fields directly (no need _determine_*) 244 if not v.get("group_id") and tmpl.group_id: 245 v["group_id"] = tmpl.group_id.id 246 if not v.get("target_document_item_id") and tmpl.target_document_item_id: 247 v["target_document_item_id"] = tmpl.target_document_item_id.id 248 if not v.get("additional_document_ids") and hasattr(tmpl, 'additional_document_ids') and tmpl.additional_document_ids: 249 v["additional_document_ids"] = [(6, 0, tmpl.additional_document_ids.ids)] 250 if not v.get("certification_name_ids") and tmpl.certification_name_ids: 251 v["certification_name_ids"] = [(6, 0, tmpl.certification_name_ids.ids)] 252 253 # Department ticket: ensure res_id is set from target_department_id (constraint: res_id not null) 254 for v in vals_list: 255 if v.get("target_department_id") and not v.get("res_id"): 256 v["res_id"] = v["target_department_id"] 257 if not v.get("res_model_id"): 258 v["res_model_id"] = self.env["ir.model"]._get_id("hr.department") 259 260 # 1. Create the records in Odoo first 261 activities = super(MailActivity, self).create(vals_list) 262 263 # 2. Initialize the Graph Client once for the batch 264 try: 265 graph_client = GraphAPIClient(self.env) 266 except Exception as e: 267 _logger.error("Failed to initialize GraphAPIClient: %s", str(e)) 268 return activities 269 270 # 3. Loop through created activities 271 for activity in activities: 272 # Check if it's a department ticket and has a recipient 273 if activity.res_model == 'hr.department' and activity.target_department_id.dept_email: 274 subject = f"New Department Ticket in Odoo: {activity.summary or 'No Subject'}" 275 # Clean HTML formatting for the email body 276 base_url = self.env['ir.config_parameter'].sudo().get_param('web.base.url') 277 action_url = f"{base_url}/web#id={activity.res_id}&model={activity.res_model}&view_type=form" 278 # Professional HTML Email Body 279 body = f""" 280
281
282

New Department Ticket Assigned

283
284 285
286

Hello Team,

287

A new activity has been formally assigned to your department for processing:

288 289 290 291 292 293 294 295 296 297 298 299 300 305 306
Department:{activity.target_department_id.name}
Summary:{activity.summary or 'No Summary'}
Deadline: 301 302 {activity.date_deadline or 'No Deadline Set'} 303 304
307 308
309 Instructions/Notes:
310
311 {activity.note or 'No additional details provided.'} 312
313
314 315

Please use the button below to view the record details and update the status in Odoo.

316 317
318 319 View Ticket in Odoo 320 321
322
323 324
325 This is an automated notification from the ACG Department Ticket System. 326
327
328 """ 329 330 try: 331 # Execute the API call 332 graph_client.send_mail( 333 recipient_email=activity.target_department_id.dept_email, 334 subject=subject, 335 content=body 336 ) 337 except Exception as e: 338 # Log the error so the Odoo record is still saved even if email fails 339 _logger.error("Microsoft Graph API failed to send email: %s", str(e)) 340 341 return activities 342 343 def action_open_as_wizard(self): 344 """ 345 Enhanced UX: 346 Force open the activity record in a clean popup modal instead of 347 redirecting the whole page. 348 """ 349 self.ensure_one() 350 return { 351 'type': 'ir.actions.act_window', 352 'name': 'Open Activity', 353 'res_model': 'mail.activity', 354 'res_id': self.id, 355 'view_mode': 'form', 356 'view_id': self.env.ref('mail.mail_activity_view_form_popup').id, 357 'target': 'new', 358 }

content.text: Now let me check the key view files to understand the patterns:

2	    <record id="mail_activity_schedule_update_view_form" model="ir.ui.view">

2	<odoo>

53	                       decoration-success="custom_state == 'done'"

56	                    <field name="create_uid" widget="many2one_avatar_user"/>
57	                    <field name="calendar_start"/>

59	                    <field name="completed_date" optional="show"/>
60	                    <field name="user_id" widget="many2one_avatar_user"/>
61	                    <field name="date_deadline" widget="remaining_days"/>
62	                    <field name="date_done" string="Done Date" optional="hide"/>
63	                    <field name="res_model_id" optional="hide" string="Document Model"/>
64	                    <field name="create_date" optional="hide" string="Created On"/>
65	                    <field name="write_uid" widget="many2one_avatar_user" optional="hide" string="Last Updated By"/>
66	                    <field name="write_date" optional="hide" string="Last Updated On"/>
67	                    <field name="note" optional="hide" string="Description" widget="html"/>
68	                    <field name="state" optional="hide" string="Status"
69	                           decoration-danger="state == 'overdue'"

71	                           decoration-success="state == 'planned'"/>
72	                    <field name="is_postponed" optional="hide" string="Postponed" widget="boolean"/>

87	    <record id="mail_activity_view_search_inherit" model="ir.ui.view">
88	        <field name="name">mail.activity.view.search.inherit</field>
89	        <field name="model">mail.activity</field>
90	        <field name="inherit_id" ref="mail.mail_activity_view_search"/>
91	        <field name="arch" type="xml">
92	            <xpath expr="//search" position="inside">
93	                <field name="activity_tag_ids" string="Tags" filter_domain="[('activity_tag_ids', 'ilike', self)]"/>
94	                <field name="model_category"/>
95	                <separator/>
96	                <filter string="Incomplete" name="incomplete" domain="[('custom_state', '=', 'planned')]"/>
97	                <filter string="Completed" name="completed" domain="[('custom_state', '=', 'done')]"/>
98	                <separator/>
99	                <filter string="CRM" name="filter_category_crm" domain="[('model_category', '=', 'crm')]"/>
100	                <filter string="SS-Business Development" name="filter_category_ssbd" domain="[('model_category', '=', 'ss_business_development')]"/>
101	                <filter string="Project" name="filter_category_project" domain="[('model_category', '=', 'project')]"/>
102	                <filter string="Contact" name="filter_category_contact" domain="[('model_category', '=', 'contact')]"/>
103	                <filter string="HR" name="filter_category_hr" domain="[('model_category', '=', 'hr')]"/>
104	                <filter string="Helpdesk" name="filter_category_helpdesk" domain="[('model_category', '=', 'helpdesk')]"/>
105	                <filter string="Sales" name="filter_category_sale" domain="[('model_category', '=', 'sale')]"/>
106	                <filter string="Purchase" name="filter_category_purchase" domain="[('model_category', '=', 'purchase')]"/>
107	                <filter string="Inventory" name="filter_category_inventory" domain="[('model_category', '=', 'inventory')]"/>
108	                <filter string="Accounting" name="filter_category_accounting" domain="[('model_category', '=', 'accounting')]"/>

content.text:
Perfect! I have all the information needed. Let me compile the comprehensive report.

## Summary: Ticket System Wizard & Update Functionality

I've found the complete patterns for how the ticket system implements the wizard popup opening and update tracking. Here's what you need to know:

---

### 1. **The `action_open_as_wizard` Method** (ticket.py, lines 343-358)

This method forces records to open in a popup modal instead of redirecting:

```python
def action_open_as_wizard(self):
    """ 
    Enhanced UX:
    Force open the activity record in a clean popup modal instead of 
    redirecting the whole page.
    """
    self.ensure_one()
    return {
        'type': 'ir.actions.act_window',
        'name': 'Open Activity',
        'res_model': 'mail.activity',
        'res_id': self.id,
        'view_mode': 'form',
        'view_id': self.env.ref('mail.mail_activity_view_form_popup').id,
        'target': 'new',
    }
```

**Key aspects:**
- `'target': 'new'` opens it as a popup modal
- References the standard Odoo popup form view: `'mail.mail_activity_view_form_popup'`
- Single record operation with `ensure_one()`

---

### 2. **How Updates Are Added to Tickets** (ticket.py, lines 18-130)

The `MailActivityUpdate` model is the audit log that stores all updates:

```python
class MailActivityUpdate(models.Model):
    _name = 'mail.activity.update'
    _description = 'Activity Update Log'
    _order = 'date desc'

    activity_id = fields.Many2one('mail.activity', required=True, ondelete='cascade')
    date = fields.Datetime(default=fields.Datetime.now, required=True)
    user_id = fields.Many2one('res.users', default=lambda self: self.env.user)
    note = fields.Html(string='Update Note')
    attachment_ids = fields.Many2many('ir.attachment', ...)
    notify_creator = fields.Boolean(default=True)
```

**Update flow:**
- When an update is created, it triggers email notifications to the ticket creator (lines 60-130)
- Uses Microsoft Graph API to send professional HTML emails
- Creates permanent audit log records (not deleted like wizard records)

---

### 3. **Editable Lists with Row-Level Action Buttons - THE KEY PATTERN**

This is used in **multiple places** throughout the ticket system. The pattern combines `editable="bottom"` with action buttons:

#### **Pattern A: Simple action button in editable list** 
From `ticket_update_view.xml` (lines 95-116):

```xml
<field name="update_line_ids" nolabel="1">
    <list editable="bottom">
        <field name="activity_id" column_invisible="1"/>
        <field name="date" string="Date"/>
        <field name="user_id" string="User" readonly="1"/>
        <field name="note" string="Update Note"/>
        <field name="notify_creator" string="Notify Creator" widget="boolean_toggle" optional="show"/>
        <field name="attachment_ids" string="Attachments" widget="many2many_binary" optional="show"/>
        <button name="%(action_open_activity_update)d"
                type="action"
                icon="fa-external-link"
                string=""
                title="Open in popup"/>
    </list>
</field>
```

**This uses:**
- `editable="bottom"` = allows inline editing with new row at bottom
- `type="action"` on button = calls the XML action record
- `%(action_open_activity_update)d` = references the action ID, which opens a popup form
- Button is an extra column alongside editable fields

#### **Pattern B: On Department Activities (hr_department_activities_view.xml, lines 9-32)**

```xml
<field name="activity_ids" context="{...}">
    <list decoration-muted="custom_state == 'done'"
          decoration-danger="custom_state == 'cancelled'"
          default_order="date_deadline desc">
        <field name="res_model" column_invisible="True"/>
        <field name="res_model_id" column_invisible="True"/>
        <field name="activity_type_id"/>
        <field name="summary"/>
        <field name="target_department_id" placeholder="Select Department..."/>
        <field name="target_document_item_id" placeholder="Select Document..."/>
        <field name="custom_state" widget="badge" .../>
        <field name="date_deadline"/>
        <field name="user_id"/>
        <button name="action_open_as_wizard" 
                type="object" 
                icon="fa-external-link" 
                string="Open" 
                title="Open Activity Wizard" 
                class="btn-primary" 
                context="{'form_view_ref': 'mail.mail_activity_view_form_popup', 'target': 'new'}"/>
    </list>
</field>
```

**Note:** This one does NOT have `editable="bottom"` - it's read-only with an action button.

#### **Pattern C: In Tree/List View (activity_view_tree.xml, lines 26-32)**

```xml
<button name="action_open_as_wizard" 
        type="object"  
        icon="fa-external-link"
        string="Open"  
        title="Open Activity Wizard"  
        class="btn-primary" 
        context="{'form_view_ref': 'mail.mail_activity_view_form_popup', 'target': 'new'}"/>
<field name="res_model" column_invisible="1"/>
<field name="res_name" colspan="1"/>
<field name="model_category" widget="badge" .../>
<field name="group_id"/>
<field name="summary"/>
<!-- ... more fields ... -->
```

---

### 4. **Two Types of Action Buttons in Lists**

The codebase demonstrates **two different button approaches**:

| Aspect | `type="action"` | `type="object"` |
|--------|-----------------|-----------------|
| **Used for** | Calling XML action records | Calling Python model methods |
| **Syntax** | `name="%(action_id)d"` | `name="method_name"` |
| **Example** | `%(action_open_activity_update)d` | `action_open_as_wizard` |
| **Opens popup?** | Yes, if action has `target="new"` | Yes, if method returns dict with `target="new"` |
| **Popup form** | Defined in action XML record | Defined in method return dict |
| **Context support** | Via `context` attribute | Via method or context param |

---

### 5. **Supporting XML Action Records**

From `ticket_update_view.xml` (lines 81-87):

```xml
<record id="action_open_activity_update" model="ir.actions.act_window">
    <field name="name">Update Details</field>
    <field name="res_model">mail.activity.update</field>
    <field name="view_mode">form</field>
    <field name="view_id" ref="mail_activity_update_view_form"/>
    <field name="target">new</field>
</record>
```

This action is referenced in buttons as `%(action_open_activity_update)d` and provides:
- The popup form view to display
- `target="new"` to open as modal
- Specific form view reference to control the UI

---

### 6. **Key Files and Locations**

**Models:**
- `X:\03_ACG\Custom_Addons\ticket_system\models\ticket.py` - Contains `action_open_as_wizard()` method and `MailActivityUpdate` model

**Views:**
- `X:\03_ACG\Custom_Addons\ticket_system\views\ticket_update_view.xml` - Update list with action button pattern (lines 95-116)
- `X:\03_ACG\Custom_Addons\ticket_system\views\activity_view_tree.xml` - Main tree view with action button (lines 26-32)
- `X:\03_ACG\Custom_Addons\ticket_system\views\hr_department_activities_view.xml` - Department activities with button
- `X:\03_ACG\Custom_Addons\ticket_system\views\ticket_wizard_view.xml` - Form wizard with `editable="bottom"` list (lines 41-45)

---

### 7. **The Update Flow (Complete)**

1. User opens ticket in popup via `action_open_as_wizard()`
2. Popup form shows `update_line_ids` list with `editable="bottom"`
3. User adds inline notes/updates in the list rows
4. User can click the action button to open specific update in dedicated popup (more detailed view)
5. When update is created, it sends email notification to ticket creator via Graph API
6. Updates persist as audit log (not deleted like wizard records)

---

This pattern gives you the best of both worlds: **quick inline editing with the ability to open detailed forms** for complex updates without leaving the main view.
